text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both study and work. Good time management involves breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps and setting daily goals. Prioritization helps you decide which tasks are most important or urgent, so you focus on what truly matters first. Equally important is scope management—knowing when a project has grown too large and learning to say "no" to extra requests. Finally, clear communication with colleagues or clients about your progress or potential delays builds trust. By using these strategies, you can reduce stress and consistently meet your deadlines.
